title: Boat Rental in Cayman Islands: Costs and Tips for 2026
topic: boat rental tips for Cayman Islands
html:
Planning a trip to the Cayman Islands in 2026 opens up a world of turquoise water and remote cays; renting a boat from George Town harbour costs from around €300 for a half-day motorboat. To start comparing options, check boat hire Cayman Islands through BoatHire24, a global marketplace where you can rent or list boats.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in the Cayman Islands?

You can rent a boat in the Cayman Islands from €300 for a small motorboat to over €2,500 for a crewed luxury yacht. Shared tours start around €100 per person. For a breakdown, see the cost section below. You can boat hire Cayman Islands for your chosen dates.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in the Cayman Islands?

You do not need a licence if you charter with a captain, but bareboat rentals require a valid ICC or equivalent. The operator confirms exact requirements, so always check before booking.

Where to set off in the Cayman Islands

George Town harbour on Grand Cayman is the main departure point, with marinas like Yacht Club and Morgan's Harbour offering secure mooring. From there, it is a 15 nautical mile run to Stingray City on the North Sound sandbar. The sister islands, Cayman Brac and Little Cayman, have quieter anchorages such as South Hole Sound and Bloody Bay Wall. Camana Bay and Seven Mile Beach also provide easy access for day trips.

Which boat to rent

  1. Centre-console motorboat, best for fishing and snorkelling trips, carries up to 6 guests, from €350 half-day
  2. Catamaran, spacious for group charters, ideal for sunset cruises, up to 12 guests, from €900 half-day
  3. Bowrider speedboat, perfect for watersports and exploring shallow cays, up to 8 guests, from €300 half-day
  4. Luxury motor yacht, crewed with full amenities, a 26m Maiora sleeps 8, from €2,500 full-day
  5. Axopar 28 Sun Top, a modern crossover for coastal cruising, up to 9 guests, from €450 half-day, see the Limpopo listing for details

Costs of hiring a boat

  • Shared group tour: from €100 per person for a two-hour guided trip
  • Small motorboat half-day (4, 5 hours): from €300 to €500
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): from €1,200 to €1,800
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day: from €2,000 to €3,500

When to rent a boat

The high season runs from December to April, when the north-east trade winds ensure calm seas and bright skies. Summer months (June to November) can still yield good weather but bring a higher chance of tropical showers. A local skipper noted:

In summer, leave early to catch the morning glass and avoid afternoon squalls that can whip up from the south-west.

At a glance

  • Price band: €300, €3,500 per day
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Trip length: half-day (4 h) or full-day (8 h)
  • Capacity: 6, 12 guests typical
  • Currency: EUR quoted, but USD accepted locally

Questions about boat rental in the Cayman Islands

Can I rent a boat without a captain?

Yes, you can rent a bareboat if you hold a valid International Certificate of Competence, but most first-time visitors opt for a skippered charter.

What is the best trip from George Town?

A full-day cruise to Stingray City and the barrier reef covers around 20 nautical miles and is the most popular itinerary.

Are there fuel costs included in the rental price?

Fuel is usually not included for bareboat rentals but is often covered in crewed charters; always confirm with the operator.

Can I anchor overnight in the Cayman Islands?

Overnight anchoring is allowed in designated areas such as South Hole Sound on Little Cayman, but you must seek a permit for some moorings.

From George Town to North Sound, Cayman waters offer endless variety. If you own a boat in the Caribbean, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach renters worldwide. For a full selection of vessels, browse boat hire Cayman Islands on the platform.
